steps.title: Name the missing measure
steps.content: The research question asks whether perceived category similarity predicts purchasing brand extensions. The researchers already calculated purchase-probability changes for each extended-brand pair, so they still need a measure of how similar consumers think the paired categories are.

steps.title: Select the direct correlation
steps.content: B measures similarity within a category, not between the extended-brand categories. C and D measure brand recognition and then correlate it with purchases or cost, which does not test perceived category similarity. The answer is A.
